title: Bitter Light Egyptian Beans Stew
description: A subtle, adventurous stew that balances the earthy bitterness of dandelion greens with the heartiness of chickpeas in a light, sous‑vide‑cooked broth.

ingredients.name: low FODMAP vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: homemade or store‑bought, no onion or garlic

ingredients.name: nutritional yeast
ingredients.notes: optional for extra umami and slight bitterness

instructions: 1. Preheat the sous‑vide water bath to 80 °C (176 °F).
2. In a heat‑proof bag, combine chickpeas, cumin, smoked paprika, turmeric, coriander, black pepper, and a pinch of salt. Seal the bag using the water‑displacement method.
3. Place the bag in the water bath and cook for 2 hours to infuse the spices into the beans, gently breaking them apart as they cook.
4. While the beans cook, heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the lemon juice and stir.
5. Once the sous‑vide cycle is complete, remove the bag, squeeze out the beans, and transfer them to the saucepan. If any liquid remains, discard it.
6. Add the vegetable broth to the saucepan and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
7. Add the chopped dandelion greens and simmer for an additional 5 minutes until the greens are bright and tender.
8. Stir in nutritional yeast, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and remove from heat.
9. Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired. Enjoy a light, bitter, and FODMAP‑friendly Egyptian experience!
